title: बगलामुखी कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीबगलामुखीकवचम् ॥

श्रीनारद उवाच —
भगवन् सर्वधर्मज्ञ कवचं बगलामुखी।
कथयस्व महाप्राज्ञ येन सिद्धिर्भवेन्नृणाम्॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु नारद वक्ष्यामि कवचं सर्वसिद्धिदम्।
बगलामुखी देव्या गोप्यं ब्रह्माण्डदुर्लभम्॥

ॐ ह्लीं शिरो मे पातु सर्वदा बगलामुखी।
ह्लीं बगलामुखी भालं पातु मे सर्वसिद्धिदा॥१॥

ॐ ह्लीं मे नेत्रयुग्मं च पातु वक्त्रं तथैव च।
कर्णौ पातु महाविद्या नासिकां पातु स्तम्भिनी॥२॥

ओष्ठौ पातु महादेवी दन्तान् पातु महेश्वरी।
चिबुकं पातु वारही गलं पातु वशंकरी॥३॥

स्कन्धौ पातु शिवा नित्यं भुजौ पातु तपस्विनी।
करौ पातु सुरेश्वरी करांगुलीः सुरार्चिता॥४॥

स्तनौ पातु महामाया हृदयं पातु योगिनी।
नाभिं पातु महालक्ष्मीः कटिं पातु सुरेश्वरी॥५॥

गुह्यं पातु महामारी ऊरू पातु शिवप्रिया।
जानुनी पातु चामुण्डा जंघे पातु बलप्रदा॥६॥

गुल्फौ पातु धराधात्री पादौ पातु सुशक्तिनी।
सर्वाङ्गं पातु सर्वेशी सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी॥७॥

प्राच्यां पातु महादेवी आग्नेय्यां पातु शांकरी।
दक्षिणे पातु वारही नैर्ऋत्यां पातु खेचरी॥८॥

पश्चिमे पातु चामुण्डा वायव्यां विश्ववन्दिता।
उत्तरे पातु कौमारी ऐशान्यां पातु शूलिनी॥९॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु महालक्ष्मीः अधः पातु महेश्वरी।
दशदिक्षु सदा पातु बगला स्तम्भिनी मम॥१०॥

इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा यो जपेद् बगलामुखीम्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिः कल्पकोटिशतैरपि॥

य इदं पठते नित्यं बगलाकवचं शुभम्।
तस्य शत्रुक्षयो नित्यं रक्षा सर्वत्र जायते॥

॥ इति श्रीबगलामुखीकवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Baglamukhi Kavacham ||

Shri Naarada Uvaacha —
Bhagavan Sarvadharmajnya Kavacham Baglamukhi.
Kathayasva Mahaapraajnya Yena Siddhirbhavennrinaam.

Shri Bhagavaan Uvaacha —
Shrinu Naarada Vakshyaami Kavacham Sarvasiddhidam.
Baglamukhi Devyaa Gopyam Brahmaandadurlabham.

Om Hleem Shiro Me Paatu Sarvadaa Baglamukhi.
Hleem Baglamukhi Bhaalam Paatu Me Sarvasiddhidaa.||1||

Om Hleem Me Netrayugmam Cha Paatu Vaktram Tathaiva Cha.
Karnau Paatu Mahaavidyaa Naasikaam Paatu Stambhinee.||2||

Oshthau Paatu Mahadevee Dantaan Paatu Maheshvaree.
Chibukam Paatu Vaaraahee Galam Paatu Vashankaree.||3||

Skandhau Paatu Shivaa Nityam Bhujau Paatu Tapasvinee.
Karau Paatu Sureshvaree Karaanguleeh Suraarchitaa.||4||

Stanau Paatu Mahaamaayaa Hridayam Paatu Yoginee.
Naabhim Paatu Mahaalakshmih Katim Paatu Sureshvaree.||5||

Guhyam Paatu Mahaamaaree Ooroo Paatu Shivapriyaa.
Jaanunee Paatu Chaamundaa Janghe Paatu Balapradaa.||6||

Gulphau Paatu Dharaadhaatree Paadau Paatu Sushaktinee.
Sarvaangam Paatu Sarveshee Sarvasiddhi-Pradaayinee.||7||

Praachyaam Paatu Mahadevee Aagneyaam Paatu Shaankaree.
Dakshine Paatu Vaaraahee Nairityaam Paatu Khecharee.||8||

Pashchime Paatu Chaamundaa Vaayavyaam Vishvavandita.
Uttare Paatu Kaumaaree Aishaanyaam Paatu Shoolinee.||9||

Urdhvam Paatu Mahaalakshmih Adhah Paatu Maheshvaree.
Dashadikshuu Sadaa Paatu Bagalaa Stambhinee Mama.||10||

Idam Kavachamajnaatvaa Yo Japed Baglamukeem.
Na Tasya Jaayate Siddhih Kalpakotishataairapi.

Ya Idam Pathate Nityam Bagalaakavacham Shubham.
Tasya Shatrukshayo Nityam Rakshaa Sarvatra Jaayate.

|| Iti Shri Baglamukhi Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्रीनारद बोले — हे भगवन्! सर्वधर्मज्ञ! बगलामुखी का कवच बताइए जिससे मनुष्यों को सिद्धि हो।

श्रीभगवान बोले — हे नारद! सुनो, सर्वसिद्धिद, बगलामुखी देवी का गोपनीय, ब्रह्माण्ड में दुर्लभ कवच कहता हूँ।

१. ॐ ह्लीं बगलामुखी सदा मेरे शिर की रक्षा करें। ह्लीं बगलामुखी सर्वसिद्धिदा मेरे भाल (ललाट) की रक्षा करें।

२. ॐ ह्लीं मेरे नेत्र-युग्म और मुख की रक्षा करें। कानों की महाविद्या, नासिका की स्तम्भिनी रक्षा करें।

३. ओष्ठों की महादेवी, दन्तों की महेश्वरी, चिबुक (ठोड़ी) की वाराही, गले की वशंकरी रक्षा करें।

४. कन्धों की शिवा नित्य, भुजाओं की तपस्विनी, हाथों की सुरेश्वरी, उंगलियों की सुरार्चिता रक्षा करें।

५. स्तनों की महामाया, हृदय की योगिनी, नाभि की महालक्ष्मी, कटि की सुरेश्वरी रक्षा करें।

६. गुह्य की महामारी, जंघाओं की शिवप्रिया, घुटनों की चामुण्डा, पिण्डलियों की बलप्रदा रक्षा करें।

७. गुल्फों (टखनों) की धराधात्री, पैरों की सुशक्तिनी, सर्वाङ्ग की सर्वेशी सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी रक्षा करें।

८. पूर्व में महादेवी, आग्नेय में शांकरी, दक्षिण में वाराही, नैर्ऋत्य में खेचरी रक्षा करें।

९. पश्चिम में चामुण्डा, वायव्य में विश्ववन्दिता, उत्तर में कौमारी, ईशान में शूलिनी रक्षा करें।

१०. ऊर्ध्व में महालक्ष्मी, अधः में महेश्वरी — दसों दिशाओं में स्तम्भिनी बगला सदा मेरी रक्षा करें।

इस कवच को जाने बिना जो बगलामुखी का जप करे, उसे कोटि-कोटि कल्पों में भी सिद्धि नहीं होती। जो नित्य इस शुभ बगला-कवच का पाठ करता है, उसके शत्रुओं का नित्य क्षय होता है और सर्वत्र रक्षा होती है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीbaglamukhikavachamमन्त्रस्य श्रीनारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री बगलामुखी (दश महाविद्या) देवता। ॐ ह्लीं बीजम्। स्तम्भन-कवच शक्तिः। शत्रुक्षय एवं सर्वरक्षा कीलकम्। श्रीबगलामुखीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Divine armor of Baglamukhi Mahavidya — protects every body part and all ten directions
Essential prerequisite for Baglamukhi mantra japa — without it siddhi does not manifest
Provides total protection from enemies who are destroyed completely
Narrated by Bhagavan to Narada — extremely rare and secret
Invokes multiple forms of the Goddess for comprehensive shield
Invokes siddhi in baglamukhi sadhana when recited before japa
Most powerful Tantric kavach for enemy destruction and self-protection
description: Baglamukhi Kavacham (बगलामुखी कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection dedicated to Goddess Durga. Drawn from Tantrasara, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to provide total protection from enemies who are destroyed completely, narrated by Bhagavan to Narada — extremely rare and secret, and grant Siddhi in Baglamukhi sadhana when recited before japa. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Navratri and Baglamukhi Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Baglamukhi Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Baglamukhi Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armor of Mahavidya Baglamukhi for total protection and enemy destruction.
relatedSearches: Baglamukhi Kavacham lyrics in Sanskrit Devanagari PDF
Pitambara Peeth Datia Madhya Pradesh temple
Baglamukhi for court case victory remedy

seoFaqs.question: What is the Baglamukhi Kavacham and which goddess does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Baglamukhi Kavacham is a Sanskrit kavacha (protective armour) prayer dedicated to Goddess Baglamukhi, the eighth of the Dasha Mahavidyas (ten Tantric wisdom-goddesses) and the supreme deity of stambhana — the Tantric power to paralyse, immobilise or silence enemies, lawsuits, slanderers and adverse forces. The kavacha encloses each limb of the chanter in Baglamukhi's protective grace, making the devotee impervious to attack and granting the same stambhana power over opponents. It is preserved in the Tantrasara, the Mantra Mahodadhi, the Rudrayamala Tantra and various Shakta nibandha collections. (Sources: Tantrasara; Mantra Mahodadhi; Stotra Nidhi Baglamukhi Kavacham.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Baglamukhi among the Dasha Mahavidyas?
seoFaqs.answer: Baglamukhi (also spelled Bagalamukhi, literally 'one whose face has the power of the bagala/bridle') is the eighth Mahavidya, depicted as a yellow-complexioned goddess seated on a golden throne in the midst of an ocean of nectar, dressed in yellow garments, ornamented with yellow flowers and gems. With her left hand she grasps the tongue of a demon-enemy, with her right hand she lifts a club to strike his head — iconic imagery of stambhana, the immobilisation of speech and action. She is also called Pitambara (yellow-clothed) and Brahmastra-vidya (the divine weapon of paralysis). Her worship is exclusively Tantric and traditionally requires diksha. (Sources: Hindupedia Dasha Mahavidya; Wikipedia Bagalamukhi; Tantrasara.)

seoFaqs.question: Does Baglamukhi Kavacham require Tantric initiation (diksha) to chant?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Tantric authorities — Mantra Mahodadhi, Tantrasara, Shakta Pramoda — emphatically require diksha (Tantric initiation) from a qualified guru in the Baglamukhi-paramparas before undertaking the kavacha or the mool mantra (Om hleem baglamukhi sarva dushtanam vacham mukham padam stambhaya jihvam kilaya buddhim vinashaya hleem om svaha). Without proper initiation and the protective rituals (purascharana) the practice can produce reverse effects, since Baglamukhi is a fierce stambhana-deity. Mainstream householders may safely chant simple Baglamukhi prayers and the bija mantra Hleem in worship at established Baglamukhi temples (Datia Pitambara Peeth, Nalkheda) under priestly guidance. The kavacha proper is best undertaken with diksha. (Sources: Tantrasara; Hindupedia Mahavidya diksha; Pitambara Peeth Datia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Baglamukhi mool mantra and how is it used with the kavacha?
seoFaqs.answer: The Baglamukhi mool mantra is Om hleem baglamukhi sarva dushtanam vacham mukham padam stambhaya jihvam kilaya buddhim vinashaya hleem om svaha — invoking the goddess to immobilise the speech, mouth and feet of all evildoers, paralyse their tongue and destroy their (hostile) intellect. The single bija is Hleem. Sadhakas chant the mool mantra preceded by Om hleem 1,008 or 10,008 times during purascharana, and bracket the kavacha recitation with mool mantra japa. Traditional procedure requires a haldi (turmeric) mala, yellow garments, yellow asana, and yellow naivedya (turmeric rice, banana, yellow sweets) in front of a Baglamukhi yantra. (Sources: Mantra Mahodadhi; Stotra Nidhi Baglamukhi sadhana; Datia Pitambara Peeth.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Baglamukhi Kavacham be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday is the primary day for Baglamukhi (some traditions also recommend Thursday), with brahma-muhurta and the Rahukala (paradoxically used here for Tantric stambhana sadhanas) being the prescribed hours. Special days include Baglamukhi Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Ashtami), the entire Vaishakha and Magha months, Navaratri (especially the eighth night dedicated to Mahagauri-Baglamukhi correspondence), every Tuesday Ashtami combination, and lunar eclipses. Devotees facing court cases, election battles, business rivals, slander, black magic, abhichara attacks, exam interviews or any opposition that needs to be silenced are advised to undertake forty-day mandalas under guru guidance. (Sources: Drikpanchang Baglamukhi Jayanti; Hindupedia Mahavidya days; Datia Pitambara Peeth.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Baglamukhi Kavacham phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the kavacha grants stambhana siddhi (the power to paralyse and silence opponents), victory in lawsuits and litigation, success in elections and political contests, defeat of business and academic rivals, removal of black magic and tantric attacks, protection during travel, and immunity from slander. Because Baglamukhi is also called Brahmastra-vidya (the Brahmastra weapon-knowledge), the kavacha is regarded as an unfailing defensive shield in matters of conflict. Tradition emphasises that these powers are granted only to those who undertake the sadhana with proper diksha, a yama-niyama lifestyle, and intentions aligned with dharma — adharmic use of stambhana boomerangs on the practitioner. (Sources: Tantrasara phalashruti; Pitambara Peeth Datia; Hindupedia Baglamukhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the major Baglamukhi temples in India?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prominent Baglamukhi temples are: Pitambara Peeth at Datia in Madhya Pradesh (the most famous, established in the twentieth century by Sri Swamiji Maharaj, regularly visited by politicians, judges and military officials seeking stambhana victory); the ancient Baglamukhi temple at Nalkheda in Agar Malwa district, Madhya Pradesh (regarded as a swayambhu shrine consecrated by the Pandavas); the Bankhandi Baglamukhi temple in Kangra, Himachal Pradesh; the Baglamukhi temple at Pathankot, Punjab; and shrines within the Kamakhya Mahavidya circuit in Assam. The Datia and Nalkheda temples are the principal pilgrimage centres for serious sadhakas. (Sources: Wikipedia Bagalamukhi temples; Hindupedia Baglamukhi pithas; Pitambara Peeth Datia.)
